Ad Text:
Your impact

We are seeking an experienced Senior Project Controls Professional to join our dynamic Project and Program Delivery Solutions team in Australia. This exciting role comes at a time of significant growth, as we've recently secured major projects across a variety of sectors. You'll have the opportunity to work on a diverse portfolio of innovative initiatives, contributing to some of the most impactful infrastructure developments in the country.

Drive delivery certainty by providing robust cost, schedule, and risk management across diverse projects in energy & power, resources, water and transport
Enhance decision making through accurate forecasting, performance reporting and data driven insights that keep projects on track and aligned with client objectives
Enable cross-sector excellence by applying best-practice project cost controls frameworks that improve efficiency and consistency
Support sustainable outcomes by optimising resources, budgets, and schedules to help deliver innovative, resilient and future-focused infrastructure
Foster collaboration and accountability by partnering with project managers, engineers, and clients to maintain transparency and achieve successful project outcomes

What you will do 

Prepare and deliver accurate monthly cost reports, variance and trend analysis, cost-to-complete projections, and cash flow forecasts to senior leadership
Establish and maintain the performance measurement baseline (PMB) by integrating scope, schedule, risk and cost baselines across all project control accounts including mapping of cost data, budgets and forecasts to WBS elements
Own the cost change management control process end-to-end: scope change identification, impact assessment, baseline re- alignment, and formal approval documentation
Collaborate with schedulers, risk managers, project managers and commercial teams to ensure integrated project controls and consistent project performance reporting
Drive cost planning and budget development during early project phases including design, procurement, and pre- construction
Operate within enterprise cost management systems (EcoSys, InEight, PRISM, or equivalent PPM/PMIS platforms) and champion data integrity and reporting standardisation
Lead cost forecasting using earned value techniques to provide early warning of cost variances  
Support commercial teams in validating invoices, contractor claims and variations/changes, manage accruals, NEC4 contract management, etc.

Here's what you'll need

We are seeking a professional with extensive experience managing day-to-day project cost controls and activities across multiple programs and projects. You should have a proven track record of project cost controls and supporting bid teams with pricing, commercial aspects, and scheduling for submissions. We're looking for someone who has mentored junior personnel in cost controls, planning and the use of relevant software such as PRISM, InEight, Ecosys or Primavera P6. Experience in promoting project controls capabilities to internal and external clients is essential, as is proficiency in conducting quantitative cost risk analysis (QSRA),

The ideal candidate will have:  #LI-KH1

A relevant tertiary degree in Engineering, Quantity Surveying, Project Management, or equivalent
Extensive experience with project controls systems and software, including cost control systems.
Strong analytical skills and the ability to provide data-driven insights
Excellent communication and interpersonal skills to collaborate effectively with diverse stakeholders
A proven track record of successful project delivery in energy & power, resources, water, or transport sectors
While not essential, Australian citizenship would be beneficial for applicants.

This role is responsible for delivering a small portfolio of high‑priority strategic projects (2-4 at any given time) that directly support NobleOak’s growth and transformation agenda, including AI‑enabled initiatives where relevant.

The Project Manager takes a hands‑on, ownership‑driven approach, building momentum and driving outcomes through practical planning, clear operating rhythms, and strong stakeholder alignment. A substance‑over‑form mindset is essential - prioritising real delivery, clear communication, and measurable business outcomes over excessive reporting or documentation.




Key Accountabilities

Project Delivery

Lead end-to-end delivery of assigned projects from initiation through to implementation and handover
Develop and maintain project plans, milestones, dependencies, and delivery schedules
Drive execution against agreed scope, timelines, and outcomes, initiating corrective action as required

Governance and Disciplines

Apply NobleOak’s project management frameworks, rhythms, and governance requirements
Facilitate effective delivery forums including SteerCos, working groups, sprint ceremonies, and reviews
Ensure appropriate documentation, approvals, and decision trails are maintained

Stakeholder and Vendor Management

Engage and influence stakeholders across business, technology, and leadership levels
Coordinate internal teams and external vendors to ensure aligned and timely delivery
Proactively manage competing priorities, constraints, and delivery risks

Risk, Budget, and Resource Management

Identify and manage delivery risks, issues, and dependencies, escalating where required
Track project budgets and resource utilisation, ensuring financial discipline
Implement mitigation strategies to protect delivery outcomes

Change and Business Adoption

Assess change impacts and support implementation of new processes, controls, and ways of working
Partner with business leaders to drive adoption and embed sustainable outcomes
Support transition of delivered initiatives into business-as-usual operations

AI Embedding

Assess business requirements for AI and Technology scoping and resourcing
Coach AI champions and wider business on AI tooling and opportunities
Work with the IT and People teams to identify and provide appropriate training, skilling and capability where required

Key Responsibilities

1. Project Leadership: Take full accountability for planning, execution, delivery, and closure of assigned projects, ensuring objectives are achieved and benefits realised.

2. Deliver against agreed metrics and OKRs, maintaining a strong focus on quality outcomes, operational efficiency, and exceptional customer service across all project activities.

3. Champion NobleOak’s high-performance culture and values, fostering positivity, integrity, and collaboration while building an inclusive, engaged, and growth-oriented team environment.

4. Drive continuous improvement in processes, systems, and reporting to strengthening project effectiveness and transparency.




Capabilities

1. Able to roll up your sleeves to develop and execute strategies, understand target audiences, and deliver purposeful messaging.

2. Thrive in fast-paced, ambiguous environments, managing shifting priorities with confidence and a strong solutions focus.

3. Demonstrated ability to take initiative, work at pace, and balance quality with delivery - even with limited support.

4. Strong business acumen, excellent leadership, and proven ability to influence at executive levels. Skilled in team building, consulting, negotiation, and facilitation.

5. Highly service-oriented with exceptional communication, interpersonal, and relationship management skills.

6. Positive, ‘can-do’ attitude coupled with emotional intelligence and a commitment to continuous learning.

7. Deep knowledge of project delivery best practices, governance, and risk management in regulated environments.

8. Experienced with delivery tools, data, analytics, and reporting to drive visibility, insight, and efficiency.

9. Strong attention to detail with a consistent focus on quality, compliance, and sustainable outcomes.

Job Title: Data Operations Analyst - Data Platform/ GCP BQ
Ad Text:
Company Description

Data Operations Analyst

12m FTC 

ASAP start 

Sydney, Hybrid (Barangaroo) 

Endeavour Group runs the country’s largest portfolio of licensed hotels, ALH Hotels, as well as Australia’s largest drinks network under the Dan Murphy’s and BWS brands plus ancillary businesses.

Endeavour Group demerged from Woolworths in 2021 and as part of the transition is taking the opportunity to transform how we operate as an independent business and establish supporting technology via the One Endeavour Transformation Program.

Within the One Endeavour Transformation Program, the “Data Program” has been stood up to transition the existing Retail Enterprise Data Warehouse from Woolworths to Endeavour.

Be uniquely you, come as you are
Bring your passion and feel the energy

The Data Operations Analyst is a key member of the Data Program team, responsible for ensuring the successful transition of the Data Platform into the BAU Data Operations Team, who monitor, triage, and resolve issues on the Data Platform.

This is a hands-on role, working closely with our BAU Technology Service Delivery Team, BAU Data Operations Team and the Data Program Team to facilitate the service transition of the Data Platform and ensure that it aligns with our Enterprise standards, processes and tools and is an optimised user experience for both the end user raising tickets and the resolver groups managing the platform stability.

Job Description

Sound good? Read on.

Here is a taster of what you can expect in this role:

Effective service transition of the Data Platform into our BAU Data Operations Team, including documentation, processes, knowledge sharing and tooling configuration. 

Design (in collaboration with the BAU service design team) the data operations processes for the transitioned platform

Document and knowledge share the newly transitioned data platform to the BAU Data Operations Team 

Configure our service management tool, and/or work with our Service Management Team to implement the Support/Operations processes on handover to BAU 

Participate in agile ceremonies, where appropriate

Qualifications

Now let’s talk about you:

Required Qualifications, Experience & requirements:

Experience supporting data warehouse/data product/ml products 

Technology operations management experience, working in complex environments 

Understanding of ITIL 

Experience with ITSM tools and processes

Experience with Cloud Data Platforms and warehouses

Strong SQL skills and understanding of ETL processes

Strong understanding of data pipelines and workflows

Knowledge of data governance and security principles

Experience using Agile tools like Atlassian JIRA, Confluence

Desirable:

Retail industry experience

Experience with ServiceNow

Understanding of Machine Learning workflow, streaming pipeline is a plus

Familiarity with Terraform or other infrastructure-as-code tools is a plus

Strong Google Cloud Platform skills in BigQuery, IAM, Dataflow, Pub/Sub, Cloud Storage, and Cloud, Function, Cloud Composer

Experience with DBT

Development experience in Python 

Understanding of different BI platforms such as Tableau, Google Looker reports

Understanding of Azure Databricks is a plus

 

 

Additional Information

The benefits are good too! 


An exclusive discount card for BWS, Dan Murphy’s, Woolworths, BIG W and other Endeavour Group brands, including our ALH pubs
 
Your health and wellbeing is your most important asset, and as one of our valued team members, it’s our first priority. You will have a range of free services to help you live well and support your physical, mental and financial wellbeing

Endeavour Group is full of opportunities - use our dedicated learning and development options to grow an idea, yourself, and your career. This is just the start, so dream big.
At Endeavour, we value being a workplace where everyone’s welcome - if you meet a number of the requirements (and not all), we encourage you to apply.

Additional Info:

We are together creators

With a portfolio that includes Dan Murphy’s, BWS, ALH Hotels, Pinnacle Drinks and more, Endeavour Group is big on sociability. Together we create the moments that bring millions of people together. And together we have more fun, create more opportunities, and score a lot more goals. We’re serious about creating a safe, inclusive and fun place to rock up to where equal opportunity is key, and flexibility is part of how we roll.  

We’re all about creating a more sociable future - for our customers and each other. If this job excites you - and you’re close-enough on the requirements, reach out, we’d love to hear from you.

You can learn more about working with us on LinkedIn or at endeavourgroupcareers.com.au.

Our Talent Team and Hiring Leaders kindly request no unsolicited resumes or approaches from Recruitment Agencies. Endeavour Group is not responsible for any fees related to unsolicited resumes.

This is a 6‑month contract for an experienced Salesforce Analyst / SME focused on cyber resilience and platform security.

Play a critical role in a cyber resilience program within a complex enterprise Salesforce environment. Based in Melbourne with hybrid flexibility, this role sits at the intersection of cyber security, platform governance, and hands‑on Salesforce configuration.

You’ll work closely with cyber security and application leaders to uplift Salesforce security posture, remediate vulnerabilities, and strengthen authentication, access, and monitoring controls. The work is practical, visible, and directly tied to reducing risk across a core commercial platform.

If you enjoy deep technical ownership, meaningful security outcomes, and collaborating with senior stakeholders to get things done properly, this is a well‑positioned contract worth serious consideration.

This is: what you’ll do
Lead remediation of Salesforce security health check findings across multiple domains
Deliver vulnerability assessment and remediation using SaaS security tooling
Collaborate with cyber security and application leads to prioritise and address risk
Configure authentication and access controls, including MFA and IP restrictions
Design and enforce least‑privilege access models using profiles and permission sets
Review and harden connected apps, OAuth usage, and API integrations
Strengthen data protection controls to prevent unauthorised access and data exfiltration
Support improved security monitoring, alerting, and documentation standards

This is: what you’ll need
Strong Salesforce security experience across enterprise environments
Hands‑on capability with org hardening, access controls, and data security models
Experience reviewing connected apps, OAuth flows, and integration security patterns
Knowledge of vulnerability management tools such as AppOmni or similar platforms
Salesforce Certified Administrator and or Platform Identity and Access Management Architect certification
Confident use of JIRA, Confluence, Microsoft tools, and process documentation
Clear communication skills and an outcomes‑focused, detail‑driven mindset

This is: the perks
Direct ownership of Salesforce security uplift and risk reduction
High‑impact work within a visible cyber resilience program
Close collaboration with senior cyber and application leaders
A focused contract delivering tangible, career‑defining outcomes

Ad Text:
About Fitness & Lifestyle Group




Fitness and Lifestyle Group (FLG) is Asia-Pacific's largest diversified fitness, health and wellness group, headquartered in Australia. We truly make a meaningful, positive impact on people's lives through innovative and diversified fitness, health and wellness offerings across physical and digital platforms. Put simply, our purpose is to empower our global community to live stronger and happier lives.




Our portfolio of globally recognised brands includes Fitness First Australia, Barry's Bootcamp, Goodlife Health Clubs Australia, Jetts, and Zap. With 300+ physical locations across Australia, New Zealand, Singapore, and Thailand in addition to its on-demand digital health and fitness programs, FLG currently helps almost a million people live healthier and more active lives.







About the Opportunity 




This is a rare opportunity to step into a pivotal leadership role as FLG embarks on a large-scale, multi-national digital transformation. 




As Head of Product Delivery, you will shape and deliver the digital experiences that connect millions of customers to our brands. Sitting at the intersection of product, technology, and commercial performance, this role is focused on building and evolving a seamless customer ecosystem that drives acquisition, conversion, and long-term engagement.




Reporting to the Group CTO, you will lead a high-impact portfolio of digital products across mobile, web, and emerging channels. From enhancing how customers discover and join, to embedding AI-driven experiences across sales and service, you will play a key role in modernising the end-to-end customer journey and unlocking new opportunities for growth.




You will lead a team of System and Product Analysts, working closely with an offshore development partner to deliver at pace across a broad range of initiatives. This is a hands-on leadership role where you will stay close to the work, guiding delivery, shaping decisions, and ensuring products are built with both customer and commercial outcomes in mind.




Operating in a fast-moving environment, you will bring clarity, momentum, and strong judgement to evolving priorities, with a clear focus on building experiences that keep our members connected, engaged, and coming back.

 

 

Key Responsibilities 




Own and execute the digital roadmap across mobile apps, websites, and ecommerce platforms

Translate business strategy into digital priorities that drive growth, engagement, and retention

Lead the development and continuous improvement of customer-facing digital products

Optimise ecommerce performance across acquisition funnels, checkout, and post-purchase journeys

Balance speed of delivery with platform scalability, data integrity, and governance

Establish strong delivery frameworks, prioritisation, and agile ways of working

Lead internal teams and external partners to deliver high-quality outcomes at pace

Own digital performance, including conversion, revenue contribution, and platform adoption

Leverage data and AI insights to continuously improve customer journeys and investment decisions

 




Skills and Experience 

Senior leadership experience in digital, platform, or technology roles within revenue-driven, customer-centric organisations. 

Proven ownership of digital channels that directly support sales and service outcomes, including apps, websites, and ecommerce.  

Experience implementing AI-driven capabilities across lead management, sales enablement, or service operations 

Strong background in sales pipeline management, revenue operations, and commercial systems enablement. 

Experience implementing AI-driven solutions across sales, service, or customer operations. 

Demonstrated ownership of ecommerce, digital acquisition, and conversion platforms.   

Experience delivering modern billing and/or payment solutions 

Strong understanding of data governance, reporting, analytics, and performance management. 

Track record of delivering enterprise-scale transformation programs involving multiple legacy systems. 

 




Personal Attributes 

Commercially focused, pragmatic, and outcomes driven. 

Comfortable operating in complex, fast-moving environments with evolving priorities. 

Equally confident setting strategy and leading hands-on execution. 

Highly accountable, with a strong sense of ownership and integrity. 

Curious about emerging technologies, with a clear focus on business value. 

Collaborative leader who builds trust across technical and commercial teams. 

Resilient, adaptable, and motivated by meaningful, large-scale impact. 




In return we offer you




Complementary all access Goodlife Gym Membership, plus one for a friend 

FLG is committed to promoting a healthy work-life balance. We offer a hybrid work model, mixing 60/40 office (Monday, Tuesday and Thursday) and work-from-home responsibilities.   

Modern open-plan office with onsite cafe and complimentary barista coffee

Anniversary leave days and additional complimentary memberships through FLG's Recognition of Service Program   

Be recognised and rewarded by your peers for living our values with FLG's People's Champion awards   

Enjoy exclusive employee discounts with industry leading fitness, travel, fashion and retail brands through FLG's partner discount program 

Leading paid parental leave program 

Employee Assistance Program




Our commitment to diversity & inclusion

At Fitness and Lifestyle Group we bring positive energy and attitude to work every day, we are proud of who we are and who we work for. We don't just accept individuality; we encourage it and acknowledge the value diversity and inclusivity brings to our organisation. We pride ourselves on empowering our team to be their authentic selves at work.




We welcome applications from Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander People, people of all ages, race, sex, intersex status, gender identity, sexual orientation, people with a disability and from other diverse backgrounds.

Play a pivotal role in shaping safe, successful enterprise transformation at Australian Retirement Trust. This is a standout opportunity to influence major change programs while providing trusted risk leadership at scale.

Why join us?

You'll get 14% super as standard, to help your future come alive.
Unleash your potential and build a career to be proud of through excellent training and development opportunities.
Balance your work between in-person days at our purpose-built hubs and working from home.
In the office, enjoy end of trip facilities, a yoga and multi-faith room, parent and carer rooms, quiet rooms, BrewHub coffee facilities, and collaborative team spaces.

About the role

As Manager – Enterprise Change Risk, you’ll provide independent second‑line risk oversight across ART’s most significant transformation initiatives, helping ensure change is delivered safely, prudently and in line with strategic objectives. Partnering closely with Transformation leaders, Executives and risk colleagues, this role plays a critical part in balancing innovation with operational resilience. You’ll bring expert insight to complex change environments, influencing decision‑making through clear risk advice and constructive challenge. Operating at pace and often amid ambiguity, you’ll help uplift risk capability and embed strong change and risk practices across the organisation. This is a senior, visible role with genuine enterprise‑wide impact.




Key aspects of the role include:

Providing strategic Line 2 risk oversight across major enterprise transformation programs
Reviewing and challenging alignment to ART’s risk, compliance and change frameworks
Partnering with change leaders and project teams to support safe and successful delivery
Advising senior leaders and Executives on risk implications associated with complex change
Contributing to executive and board‑level reporting through clear articulation of risk impacts
Participating in, and at times leading, risk governance forums related to transformation activity
Working collaboratively with first and third line risk teams to ensure enterprise alignment
Driving a strong risk culture by coaching, influencing and uplifting risk capability
Responding flexibly to shifting priorities, escalations and concurrent change initiatives

About you

It goes without saying you'll be a great communicator with top notch interpersonal skills. We'll also expect you to pick up problems and come up with quick, creative ways to solve them. It's quite likely you tick some of the following boxes too:  

Bring extensive experience in enterprise, operational or change risk within complex, regulated environments
Demonstrate deep knowledge of risk management frameworks, governance and compliance requirements
Proven ability to embed risk practices into large‑scale programs or transformation initiatives
Confidence partnering with senior leaders and Executives, providing trusted advice and constructive challenge
Strong judgement when balancing innovation, delivery pace and operational resilience
Experience contributing to executive‑level reporting and risk forums with clarity and credibility
Comfortable operating in ambiguity, managing competing priorities and responding to emerging risks
A collaborative mindset, with the ability to influence across functions and lines of defence
Commitment to building strong risk culture through leadership, coaching and capability uplift

About us

Our 2.4 million members trust us to take care of over $330 billion in retirement savings. And we help them take charge of their finances and face the future with confidence. About the role

As Manager – Enterprise Change Risk, you’ll provide independent second‑line risk oversight across ART’s most significant transformation initiatives, helping ensure change is delivered safely, prudently and in line with strategic objectives. Partnering closely with Transformation leaders, Executives and risk colleagues, this role plays a critical part in balancing innovation with operational resilience. You’ll bring expert insight to complex change environments, influencing decision‑making through clear risk advice and constructive challenge. Operating at pace and often amid ambiguity, you’ll help uplift risk capability and embed strong change and risk practices across the organisation. This is a senior, visible role with genuine enterprise‑wide impact.




Key aspects of the role include:

Providing strategic Line 2 risk oversight across major enterprise transformation programs
Reviewing and challenging alignment to ART’s risk, compliance and change frameworks
Partnering with change leaders and project teams to support safe and successful delivery
Advising senior leaders and Executives on risk implications associated with complex change
Contributing to executive and board‑level reporting through clear articulation of risk impacts
Participating in, and at times leading, risk governance forums related to transformation activity
Working collaboratively with first and third line risk teams to ensure enterprise alignment
Driving a strong risk culture by coaching, influencing and uplifting risk capability
Responding flexibly to shifting priorities, escalations and concurrent change initiatives

About you

It goes without saying you'll be a great communicator with top notch interpersonal skills. We'll also expect you to pick up problems and come up with quick, creative ways to solve them. It's quite likely you tick some of the following boxes too:  

Bring extensive experience in enterprise, operational or change risk within complex, regulated environments
Demonstrate deep knowledge of risk management frameworks, governance and compliance requirements
Proven ability to embed risk practices into large‑scale programs or transformation initiatives
Confidence partnering with senior leaders and Executives, providing trusted advice and constructive challenge
Strong judgement when balancing innovation, delivery pace and operational resilience
Experience contributing to executive‑level reporting and risk forums with clarity and credibility
Comfortable operating in ambiguity, managing competing priorities and responding to emerging risks
A collaborative mindset, with the ability to influence across functions and lines of defence
Commitment to building strong risk culture through leadership, coaching and capability uplift

Step into a role where your work directly shapes the success of a large-scale payroll transformation.

This is an opportunity for a detail-driven, hands-on data professional to play a pivotal role in migrating complex, sensitive payroll data into a new system, ensuring accuracy, integrity, and business continuity every step of the way.

You'll partner closely with a Data Lead and cross-functional teams across Payroll, HR, and IT, becoming the go-to expert for data validation, reconciliation, and insight during a critical system implementation.
 
Why this role stands out

Be at the heart of a major payroll system transformation
Work with complex, high-volume datasets that truly challenge your analytical skills
Collaborate with senior stakeholders across business and technology
Make a visible impact; your work directly influences payroll accuracy for the entire organisation
What you'll be doing
You'll take ownership of the data side of the migration, ensuring everything is accurate, reconciled, and fit for purpose:

Deep dive into payroll data; uncover patterns, anomalies, and insights across multiple sources
Partner with Payroll, HR, and Operations to validate data issues and drive resolution outcomes
Map legacy payroll data into the new system, ensuring alignment with vendor requirements
Lead post-migration validation, including reconciliation of pay components, leave balances, and historical transactions
Build and maintain error logs, reconciliation reports, and defect analysis summaries
Translate business needs into structured data requirements for front-end solutions
Analyse historical datasets to determine what data should be surfaced, transformed, or archived
Support SIT, UAT, and parallel runs with a strong focus on data integrity and defect resolution
Create clear documentation, data mappings, transformation rules, and key decisions with stakeholder sign-off
Act as a bridge between technical and non-technical teams, clearly communicating findings and insights
What you'll bring
You're a data specialist who thrives in project environments and knows how to handle complex, sensitive datasets:

Proven experience in data analysis, payroll, or transformation projects
Strong track record working with large, complex datasets and multi-step reconciliations
Advanced Excel skills
Solid understanding of data structures, relational databases, and data modelling concepts
Working knowledge of SQL for data extraction and validation
Experience building reconciliation tools, trackers, and structured reporting outputs
Exceptional attention to detail and ability to deliver under pressure
A collaborative, adaptable approach—comfortable working across multiple teams
Nice to have

Exposure to ETL tools (SSIS, Talend, Informatica, Azure Data Factory)
Experience with Power Query or BI tools
Familiarity with payroll platforms such as SAP, Dayforce, Workday, Chris21, or Preceda
Understanding of payroll compliance, audit, and data retention practices
The opportunity
This is more than just a data role, it's your chance to be part of a business-critical transformation, where your expertise ensures employees are paid accurately and systems operate seamlessly from day one.

If you enjoy solving complex data challenges and want your work to genuinely matter, this is the role for you.

About the opportunity

Fyfe is an Australian integrated engineering, environment, planning, and survey firm working at the forefront of a constantly evolving industry. As we continue to grow, were guided by shared values of collaboration, innovation, and meaningful impact.

Heritage is a key part of Fyfe's future. Our Heritage team is growing alongside our infrastructure portfolio and were looking for a Senior Heritage Consultant who wants to be part of that journey, someone who enjoys building, refining, and shaping how heritage services are delivered as the business evolves.

This Sydney based role offers the chance to contribute to a range of active heritage projects across a diverse sector, including large scale renewable energy, the advertised role will primarily support large-scale infrastructure projects across NSW and the Sydney Basin. You'll play a client facing and visible role in strengthening our heritage offering, influencing project delivery approaches, with the opportunity to grow into a leadership role, you will be supporting the development of both people and processes as the team grows.

What you'll be doing

Playing a key role in the delivery and growth of Fyfe's heritage services

Leading high-quality project delivery across a growing portfolio

Taking accountability for project quality in collaboration with the Quality Manager

Leading project planning sessions and helping shape delivery frameworks

Identifying opportunities to improve systems, processes, and efficiencies

Managing project budgets, timelines, and resourcing as the portfolio expands

Supporting and mentoring team members as the Heritage team grows

Building strong, trusted relationships with clients and internal stakeholders

About you

You're a senior heritage professional who enjoys being part of a team in growth mode. You're comfortable working at both strategic and delivery levels and are motivated by the opportunity to help shape how a team operates and evolves.

A degree in archaeology or a related discipline

Excellent communication and relationship-building skills

Experience in client relationship management

A collaborative mindset and experience working in multidisciplinary teams

Leadership capability, with an interest in mentoring and coaching others

A passion for delivering quality innovative solutions

Why Fyfe?

Clear pathways for career progression and leadership development

Flexible working arrangements to suit your lifestyle

A genuinely social, inclusive, and collaborative workplace

Ongoing focus on listening to our people and improving how we work

A full calendar of social and networking events

Purchased leave options via salary sacrifice

Data Analyst

Overview

We are seeking a hands-on Data Analyst to support a high-priority employment remediation project. This role will focus on integrating, cleansing, and validating complex workforce and payroll data from multiple legacy sources.

Depending on experience, the successful candidate may also take on lead responsibilities, including structuring the dataset and guiding other analysts.

Contract Details

Start: ASAP
Location: Melbourne CBD
Contract Length:4 Months with potential extensions.

Key Responsibilities

Integrate non-standard and fragmented datasets (payroll, HR records, legacy systems, manual inputs) into a single dataset
Clean, structure, and validate data to ensure accuracy and auditability
Identify and resolve data inconsistencies, gaps, and anomalies
Build and maintain Excel-based models and reconciliation frameworks
Support remediation calculations and reporting outputs
Collaborate with HR, Payroll, and Finance teams to validate data integrity
Work at pace to deliver against tight project timelines

For more senior candidates:

Take ownership of the overall data structure and approach
Provide direction and oversight to supporting analysts
Engage stakeholders to ensure alignment on data assumptions and outputs

Skills & Experience

Strong experience working with complex, messy, or incomplete datasets
Advanced Excel capability (data manipulation, formulas, pivots, reconciliation)
Experience in payroll, workforce data, or remediation projects highly desirable
Ability to consolidate multiple data sources into a usable format
High attention to detail with a focus on accuracy and audit requirements
Comfortable working in a fast-paced, delivery-focused environment
Proactive, hands-on mindset with a clear "get it done" approach

Our Approach to Flexible Work
 

With Flex Work, we’re combining the best of both worlds: in-person time and remote. Our approach enables our teams to deepen connections, maintain a strong community, and do their best work. We know that flexibility can take shape in many ways, so rather than a number of required days in-office each week, we simply spend at least half (50%) of our time each quarter in the office or in the field with our customers, prospects, and partners (depending on role). This means you'll have the freedom to create a flexible schedule that caters to your business, team, and personal needs, while being intentional to make the most of time spent together. Those in our remote "home office" roles also have the opportunity to come together in our offices for important moments that matter.

Australia Post is delivering for all Australians. From the vehicles that we drive, to the small businesses that make our communities thrive. We’re delivering for the environment, for our communities, for our customers and for our people. We’re moving forward and we want you to come along for the ride.  




About the opportunity  

As a Data Solutions Lead within the Freight Services team, you will own and drive the definition and delivery of data‑led solutions for priority business use cases. Working closely with business stakeholders and specialist data teams, you will identify where data and analytics can deliver the greatest value, shape clear product requirements, and prioritise delivery to enable data‑driven decision‑making across product strategy, pricing, and freight experience. You will play a key role in modernising the data landscape and establishing foundational enterprise data capabilities aligned to Freight’s long‑term vision.

The role requires strong stakeholder leadership and collaboration, operating as a trusted partner across Freight Services and the broader organisation. You will engage confidently with senior stakeholders, navigate complex delivery environments, and provide clear guidance on trade‑offs, risks, and outcomes. By combining product ownership, technical understanding of data warehousing, and delivery focus, you will help unblock teams and drive sustained progress against Freight Services’ strategic objectives. 

What you’ll deliver with us  

•            Own end‑to‑end delivery of data solutions for defined business use cases, from requirements through to implementation.

•            Prioritise delivery effectively, balancing business value, technical dependencies, delivery risk and platform constraints.

•            Assess use‑case needs and shape delivery approaches, identifying the skills and capabilities required across delivery teams.

•            Influence decision‑making at all levels, helping stakeholders understand insights and the story behind the data to support strategic planning.

•            Support strategic insight delivery and future‑state design, contributing to enterprise data solutions, tooling, strategy, and supporting frameworks.
 

About you  

You will have exceptional business and commercial acumen and a data engineering background with an understanding of data and some knowledge of how that fits in with products or large programs of work.

You’ll also need   

•            Proficient in programming languages and tools used for data engineering and governance such as SQL, Python, CI/CD deployments etc.

•            Strong hands‑on understanding of enterprise data warehouse architectures, including data ingestion, transformation, modelling, and consumption layers.

•            Highly developed understanding of data engineering, data governance, and data modelling best practices, including data quality, lineage, metadata management, and performance optimisation.

•            Experience working with cloud‑based data platforms such as Google Cloud (preferred), AWS, or Azure, including building and operating enterprise data warehouses.

•            Experience supporting analytics, reporting, or self‑service BI platforms built on top of data warehouses.
 

How we’ll deliver for you

Be part of one of the largest programs of work in logistics in Australia.
Develop your experience and thrive in a culture of collaboration.
A great benefits package and market leading culture, hybrid working from our Burnley office with great transport links.

If this sounds like you, please apply today for immediate consideration.

At Goodman Fielder, we're on a journey to build the future of food — and we're looking for an exceptional Enablement Lead to help shape where we play, how we win, and what comes next.

Lead strategic projects that shape Goodman Fielder's long‑term growth pathway.
Identify new markets, adjacencies, categories, and opportunities using structured analysis and deep insight.
Provide executive leadership with fact‑based recommendations, business cases, and investment options.
Partner cross‑functionally to ensure strategy is understood, embedded, and brought to life.
Turn strategy into clear roadmaps, priorities, and operating rhythms that drive momentum.
Support strategic reviews, transformation initiatives and performance deep‑dives.
Design and deliver enterprise planning cycles and business‑wide cadences.

Your work will directly influence the decisions we make, the investments we prioritise, and the future we build.

Customer‑oriented mindset with experience turning customer insight into strategy.

Why Goodman Fielder?

If you're looking for a role where your thinking matters and your work drives meaningful change — this is it.

Data, Automation, Integration & AI Manager Melbourne | Hybrid 

Let’s be clear, this is no BAU data role!

This is a build, fix, and lead mandate inside a serious business investing heavily in its future.

We’re partnering with a high-performing, privately-owned Australian success story undergoing a major technology and ERP transformation, and they’ve identified one critical gap:

They don’t have a single owner of data truth, integration discipline, automation strategy, and AI enablement.

That’s where you come in.

The Opportunity (Why this role actually matters) Right now:

Data is fragmented
Reporting is inconsistent
Integrations are messy
Automation exists… but not strategically
AI is being explored, but not governed
This hire will make significant changes;
You will:

Establish “Data at the Core”
Define enterprise standards across data, reporting, integration & automation
Turn Power BI into a trusted single source of truth
Move automation from tactical - to scalable services
Introduce AI properly (governed, valuable, controlled)
And most importantly - bring the business on the journey
What You’ll Own

This is a true enterprise-level leadership role without the bureaucracy.
You will:

Set and enforce data, reporting, and integration standards
Eliminate duplicate reporting, conflicting metrics, and poor data quality
Design scalable integration & automation patterns (not band-aids)
Govern change - stop bad decisions before they become technical debt
Build a capability, not just a team
Partner with Architecture, Delivery & the Business to drive real outcomes
You will also have the authority to:

Challenge poor solutions
Pause work that creates long-term risk
Reset direction when needed
(Backed by a CIO who actually wants this done properly)
What Success Looks Like (6–12 months)
A clear, trusted data model across the business
Power BI becomes the front door for decision-making
Manual processes materially reduced through automation
Integration moves from spaghetti - to structured & reusable
AI use cases delivering real business value (not hype)
A visible uplift in capability, standards, and discipline
  
The Environment
350 applications across the landscape
Microsoft-centric stack (Dynamics, Power BI, Azure direction)
Heavy integration challenges across business systems
ERP transformation underway (and needs direction)
Strong appetite for AI, automation, and better decision-making
Translation:

Plenty of complexity.
Plenty of opportunity.
No shortage of problems to solve.
Who This Suits (and who it doesn’t) You are:

About the Role

We are seeking an SAP Capital Planning Specialist to join our expanding Finance & Analytics practice. This role is responsible for designing and delivering enterprise‑grade capital planning, budgeting, forecasting, and investment modelling solutions across SAP platforms.

You will work closely with Finance, Project Management, and Asset‑Intensive business units to support multi‑year capital programs, asset lifecycles, depreciation models, portfolio prioritisation, and scenario planning.

Experience in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC Planning), SAP BPC, SAP Group Reporting, S/4HANA Finance, or SAP Capital Investment Management (IM/PS) is highly regarded.

This role can be based anywhere in Australia or New Zealand, with flexible remote or hybrid options.

Your tasks and responsibilities
Design and deliver SAP capital planning solutions, including investment planning, capital budgeting, multi‑year forecasts, and approvals workflows.
Build and enhance planning models in SAP Analytics Cloud (Planning) or other SAP EPM tools such as BPC or Group Reporting.
Configure capital planning processes including depreciation modelling, asset lifecycle planning, scenario analysis, and cash flow projections.
Work with Finance and Project/Asset teams to translate complex business requirements into scalable SAP planning solutions.
Support reporting and analytics using SAC dashboards, S/4HANA data models, and Group Reporting structures.
Optimise planning processes, improve data quality, streamline workflows, and enhance forecast accuracy.
Document models, assumptions, planning cycles, and user guidance.
Support testing, cutover, deployment, and ongoing Aftercare services.
Your qualifications and experience
Experience in capital planning, financial modelling, enterprise planning, or FP&A.
Practical experience with SAP Analytics Cloud (Planning), SAP BPC, SAP Group Reporting, or similar tools.
Strong understanding of capital project lifecycles, asset accounting, depreciation, and financial planning concepts.
Experience with SAP S/4HANA Finance (FICO), Project Systems (PS), or Investment Management (IM) is highly desirable.
Strong analytical and stakeholder engagement skills, especially with finance and operational teams.
Ability to deliver planning models for large capital portfolios and multi‑year investment cycles.
Excellent communication, documentation, and presentation skills.

Nice to have:

Experience with S/4HANA Public Cloud financial planning features.
Exposure to asset‑heavy industries (energy, utilities, mining, construction, infrastructure).
SAP certifications (SAC Planning, S/4 Finance, BPC, Group Reporting).
What we offer

Flexible and supportive work environment — remote or hybrid.
Competitive remuneration and benefits including salary packaging and development programs.
Access to SAP certifications, structured learning, and continuous enablement.
Be part of a respected SAP Platinum Partner delivering major transformations across ANZ.

Enterprise Data Standards Specialist (12-Month Contract)

Sydney, Brisbane, Hybrid | Lead enterprise data standards, governance and integrity




Introduction

Our client is seeking a highly experienced Enterprise Data Standards Specialist to play a key role in establishing and uplifting data governance, standards and integrity across a complex global organisation.

This role will provide leadership and oversight for the design and implementation of enterprise-wide data standards, frameworks and control mechanisms that ensure data is consistent, trusted and aligned to business objectives. The focus will be on strengthening data quality, governance maturity and accountability across multiple domains including Sales, Account Management, Customer, Operations and Finance, enabling high-quality insights, forecasting and commercial decision-making.

Working closely with executive stakeholders and cross-functional teams, you will guide the organisation in embedding best-practice data governance, driving behavioural change and promoting a culture of data stewardship. You will play a critical role in translating complex data dependencies into clear business outcomes, ensuring that data standards directly support customer insights, operational efficiency and commercial value creation.

This is a 12 month fixed-term engagement (with potential extension), supporting a significant enterprise-wide data uplift initiative designed to enhance data integrity, governance maturity and the organisation’s ability to leverage data as a strategic asset.


About 9X5 Consulting

We are transforming the way business and technology is managed by putting real-time data into the hands of every decision maker across organisations. The insight garnered from diverse backgrounds, perspectives and lived experiences results in pioneering innovations across the organisation and better experiences for our customers. The more diverse our talent, the more impact we have on each other and on our valued clients.

Our clients range from small SME's right through to some of the largest ASX listed organisations in Australia, so experience at selling to stakeholders at this level is required.


A bit about you

You are a senior data governance leader with a strong track record of designing and embedding enterprise data standards across complex, global organisations. You understand that high-quality, trusted data is the foundation of commercial insight, operational efficiency and customer value.

You bring a strategic mindset combined with the ability to translate complex data frameworks into practical, business-aligned outcomes. You are comfortable operating at an executive level, influencing stakeholders across Sales, Operations, Finance and Technology to drive alignment and accountability.

Exceptional communication skills are mandatory in this role. You will be required to clearly articulate complex data concepts, dependencies and risks into meaningful business impacts, ensuring engagement and adoption across diverse global teams.


Key Responsibilities                           

As the Enterprise Data Standards Specialist, you will provide strategic leadership and governance oversight across the organisation’s data landscape, ensuring enterprise data standards are designed, implemented and sustained in alignment with business and commercial objectives. You will drive the uplift of data integrity, governance maturity and accountability, enabling trusted data to underpin decision-making, customer insights and operational performance.

Your responsibilities will include:

Establish and govern enterprise-wide data standards, policies and control frameworks

Provide strategic leadership to uplift data governance maturity and capability

Drive alignment between data standards and commercial, operational and customer outcomes

Communicate data policies and insights across global and regional stakeholders

Deliver executive-level reporting on data quality, integrity and risk

Champion data stewardship and behavioural change across the organisation

Enable data-driven decision-making, forecasting and customer insights

Support platform convergence and future innovation through robust data frameworks




Key Deliverables

Cultural Adoption – Establish clear, enforceable ownership of data across global and regional operations, shifting the organisation from reactive data correction to proactive data stewardship.

Data Literacy Program – Design and implement a structured data literacy initiative that builds organisational capability, drives accountability and embeds consistent understanding of data standards across all job families.

Data Standards Framework – Develop and implement the formal structures, definitions, business rules, controls and artifacts required to ensure data is globally consistent, trusted, reusable and delivers measurable commercial value.

Data Governance Model – Establish the governance structure, authority, accountability and decision-making mechanisms required to ensure the Data Standards Framework is consistently applied, enforced and sustained across all regions and business units.

Business Process Re-Engineering (BPR) – Lead the redesign of end-to-end business processes to simplify operations, eliminate inefficiencies, strengthen data controls and improve performance, customer outcomes and overall business value.




Job Requirements

This role requires a highly experienced data governance leader who has successfully delivered enterprise-wide data standards and control frameworks within large, complex organisations.

You will be comfortable operating across global business functions, partnering with executive stakeholders and driving alignment between data governance and commercial outcomes. You will have a proven ability to influence behaviour, embed accountability and uplift data maturity across diverse teams.

Essential

10+ years’ experience in data governance, including 5+ years in senior leadership roles

Proven experience designing and implementing enterprise data standards and control frameworks

Strong stakeholder engagement skills with experience influencing executive leadership

Experience aligning data governance with commercial, operational and customer objectives

Ability to resolve complex data ownership and data quality challenges across global environments

Tertiary qualification in Information Management, Business Information Systems, Data Governance, Analytics or similar

Desirable

Experience in high-volume, global industries (travel, logistics, retail or similar)

Certifications such as CDMP, ISO 8000, ITIL 4 or COBIT

Personal attributes

To be successful in this role you will demonstrate:

Strategic thinker with strong commercial acumen

Highly influential with the ability to drive behavioural change

Strong communicator who can simplify complex concepts

Results-driven with a focus on measurable outcomes

Collaborative leader who builds trust across global teams

High attention to detail with a strong governance mindset

Deal-breakers (stuff we don’t want in a new team member)

A “that’ll do” approach to our clients’ projects;

A closed mind to change;

Big egos. We’re really, really not keen on big egos here.




This is a senior leadership role requiring both strategic oversight and hands-on delivery of enterprise data governance initiatives. Please submit your application via SEEK to be considered for this opportunity.


*Only applicants with citizenship, permanent visa will be considered.

No agencies please!

The opportunity

The Privacy Specialist plays a key role in shaping and strengthening QBE’s approach to privacy and responsible data use. Working as part of the Group Privacy Team, this role partners closely with the business to manage privacy risk, uplift capability, and embed privacy‑by‑design across data, technology and AI initiatives.

About QBE  

At QBE, we get to the heart of what matters for our customers. And we do it all with a human touch.  

We’re an international insurer with more than 13,000 people working across 26 countries – which means we’re big enough for your ambitions, yet small enough for you to make a real impact. It’s an exciting time. We’re building momentum towards our vision to become the most consistent and innovative risk partner.

What if you could have a positive impact – at work and in the world? As part of the QBE team, you’ll get to spend every day working with people who are passionate, talented and kind.

Your new role

As Privacy Specialist, you will support QBE’s Group and AUSPAC Division by providing trusted, pragmatic privacy advice while helping to evolve our privacy strategy, governance and risk management maturity. You’ll work collaboratively across the organisation, influencing outcomes and ensuring privacy considerations are embedded into business decision‑making.

Key responsibilities include:

Delivering high‑quality privacy advice, including undertaking Privacy Impact Assessments (PIAs) and managing privacy incidents, complaints and enquiries

Partnering with Technology, Data, AI and project teams to identify, assess and manage privacy, ethical and regulatory risks

Embedding privacy‑by‑design, ethical design and responsible AI principles across initiatives and programs

Supporting the implementation of Group privacy, data and AI policies within the AUSPAC business

Contributing to the continuous improvement of privacy policies, standards, training and awareness across the organisation

Supporting privacy governance, reporting, metrics and insights for senior leaders and key stakeholders

About you

You are a confident privacy professional with deep subject matter expertise and a pragmatic, solutions‑focused approach. You enjoy partnering with the business, can balance competing priorities, and are comfortable operating autonomously in a complex, fast‑paced environment. You bring strong judgement, clear communication skills and a collaborative mindset.

Key requirements:

Proven experience advising on privacy risk, Privacy Impact Assessments and incident management

Strong knowledge of privacy obligations, data ethics and emerging technologies, including AI

Experience working in large, complex organisations with diverse stakeholder groups

Demonstrated ability to prioritise, triage and manage multiple privacy matters effectively

Strong stakeholder engagement, influencing and communication skills

Experience in insurance or financial services is advantageous, but not essential

Benefits  

We offer a range of benefits to help provide holistic support for your work life, whatever your circumstances. As a QBE employee you will have access to and benefit from:

‘Hybrid Working’ – a mix of working from home and in the office to enhance your work/life balance

Free holistic wellbeing coaching, nutritional, confidential counselling, financial and legal advice

18 weeks’ gender-equal flexible leave for all new parents, including paid super

Ad Text:
At Australian Retirement Trust we’re building resilient portfolios that deliver strong, long term outcomes for our members. We’re looking for an experienced Senior Portfolio Manager – Planning to be our strategic lead for multi-year business planning across the investment business area, turning strategy into an aligned plan, clear priorities and measurable delivery.

Why join us?

You'll get 14% super as standard, to help your future come alive.
Unleash your potential and build a career to be proud of through excellent training and development opportunities.
Balance your work between in-person days at our purpose-built hubs and working from home.
In the office, enjoy end of trip facilities, a yoga and multi-faith room, parent and carer rooms, quiet rooms, BrewHub coffee facilities, and collaborative team spaces.

About the role

We’re all about helping our members make the most of their money. And while they go after their goals, you can too. This is a highly influential role at the centre of how the Investment business area sets direction and delivers.  You’ll lead the multi‑year business planning cycle, ensuring the investment business area strategic priorities translate into a coherent roadmap with clear priorities and measurable outcomes.

Reporting to the General Manager, Sustainable Investment & Planning, you’ll work closely with senior leaders across Investments and partner teams (including risk, finance, investment operations and investment legal) to align assumptions, trade‑offs and delivery commitments and provide high‑quality reporting for executive and committee forums.

Day to day, you'll:

Lead the multi‑year planning process across the Investments business area, ensuring alignment with ART’s enterprise strategy and long-term investment outcomes.
Shape strategic options and recommendations for senior leaders including prioritisation, sequencing, interdependencies and key trade‑offs.
Partner with internal stakeholders to align inputs and drive end‑to‑end development and execution of multi‑year business plans.
Oversee the delivery of strategic initiatives, including defining success measures and tracking outcomes.
Provide high‑quality insights, reports and recommendations to senior leadership and the Investment Committee on progress, outcomes, risks and opportunities.
Monitor industry, regulatory and market developments to inform strategic priorities.
Build and maintain strong relationships with internal and external stakeholders.
Represent ART on committees and at industry forums, contributing thought leadership and enhancing our brand.
Lead, develop and support a high‑performing team in an inclusive and values‑driven environment.

This role is a permanent opportunity. 

📍 Location: Sydney or Brisbane.

About you

You're a strategic thinker who can connect the dots across a complex function turning ambition into aligned, executable multi-year roadmap. You are a natural collaborator with experience in investments and the ability to influence senior stakeholders through clear, evidence-based recommendations. You’ll likely tick some of these boxes:

You hold relevant tertiary qualifications, with a degree or postgraduate qualification in finance, investments, economics, commerce, engineering or a related discipline.
You have deep experience in the investments industry, with strong knowledge of legislation, regulation, market dynamics, risk return optimisation and emerging industry trends.
You bring exceptional research and analytical capability, using data, insights and evidence to inform strategic, innovative thinking.
You’re highly skilled in stakeholder management and negotiation, and you’re comfortable influencing senior leaders and executive audiences.
You’ve successfully led and developed teams, and you’ll foster an inclusive, high performance culture that supports growth and delivery.

About us

Our 2.4 million members trust us to take care of over $330 billion in retirement savings. And we help them take charge of their finances and face the future with confidence. Our inclusive culture means you’ll be valued and heard in a respectful workplace. We've created an environment where you can be yourself and do amazing work. It's that simple.

Apply online now

Everyone is welcome to apply. We value diverse thinking, cultures, perspectives, backgrounds, and abilities. Awaken your career at Australian Retirement Trust and apply now. Applications close Sunday, 12 April 2026. 

We don’t want to leave you with a monstrous wait. So, you’ll hear from us after the closing date to find out whether you’ve made it through to the next round or not. Being fair and up-front is important to us – and recruitment is no different.

This is a senior BAU leadership role with a strong uplift mandate—ideal for someone who thrives on bringing structure, clarity and performance to complex, fast-growing organisations.

You will play a pivotal role in enabling data-driven decision making, improving system and data platform effectiveness, and ensuring high-quality reporting across a substantial and expanding portfolio.

You will also lead a team responsible for data governance, reporting and systems, ensuring these functions support delivery, performance and executive decision-making.

Key responsibilities include:

Owning enterprise data, reporting frameworks and core operational systems
Delivering high-quality daily, weekly and monthly operational reporting
Translating complex operational activity into clear, decision-ready insights
Improving system effectiveness (including CRM platforms) and reducing manual workarounds
Partnering with technology teams to define and deliver system enhancements
Establishing strong data governance, integrity and assurance frameworks
Leading external and executive reporting requirements
Building and leading a high-performing team

Current State

You will step into a function that is operationally critical but requires uplift, including:

Fragmented data across multiple platforms
Low adoption of core systems
Manual reporting processes and workarounds
Rapidly growing portfolio with significant delivery pressure

This role will be instrumental in defining problems, driving alignment, and implementing practical solutions.

About You

You are a senior data and insights professional who sits at the intersection of data, reporting, systems, and risk, and who combines strategic thinking with hands-on delivery.

Proven leadership experience in data, reporting or operational intelligence functions
Strong capability in data governance, reporting frameworks and performance insights
Experience in enhacing or implementing business-critical technology systems
Ability to operate in ambiguous environments and define solutions from first principles
Strong stakeholder engagement skills, with the ability to influence at executive level
A pragmatic, outcomes-focused approach

Why Apply?

This is a great opportunity to step into a high-impact leadership role where you will own and transform data, reporting and systems capability in a complex, growing environment.

Experience in superannuation, fund management or a custodian environment

Experience managing outsourced service providers and custodian relationships
Proven ability to lead, coach and develop teams
Strong stakeholder engagement skills, with the ability to influence at senior levels
A pragmatic, analytical and detail‑focused approach, with the ability to prioritise and meet deadlines
Strong written and verbal communication skills, including the ability to present complex financial information clearly
You'll also demonstrate UniSuper's core capabilities, including facilitating change, operational decision‑making, prioritisation and accountability, and driving performance excellence.

About Protecht

Protecht is a global risk management SaaS business. We help organizations manage risk better every day. We’re growing fast, scaling globally, and building teams that are smart, ambitious and supportive.




The role (and why it matters?)

We’re looking for a Revenue Operations Lead, to join our Sydney team. Reporting to the Global Head of Revenue Operations, you’ll own and optimize core revenue processes, systems, and data integrity across Sales, Customer Success, and Partnerships, whilst working very closely with Marketing Operations. You will drive sustainable growth across APAC, the UK and the US.

This is a visible, hands-on, and high-impact role with strong exposure to senior leaders and global teams. You’ll have real influence, autonomy and room to grow.




Why join us?

We’re proud to be certified as a Great Place to Work four years in a row. We look after our people while delivering at a high level. Here are some of the perks of working with Protecht:

Genuine career growth in a fast-growing SaaS business.

Flexible work that supports work and life - our way of working lets people work across home and in our offices.

A strong commitment to your learning and development - fortnightly dedicated L&D afternoons.

A strong focus on work / life balance with access to Birthday leave, bonus days, paid parental leave and long service leave.

Modern Sydney CBD office.

Global exposure across APAC, UK, and US teams.

Award-winning culture – Great Place to Work (4 years running).

 

About you!

You’re comfortable working at pace and dealing with complexity. You care about outcomes, collaboration and doing things properly. And you want to be part of a business that’s going places. This role suits you if you have solid experience in SaaS sales cycles and a strong interest in data, systems, and continuous process improvement.




What you’ll be doing

Go-to-Market Tech Stack

Own the configuration and long-term roadmap of core GTM systems.

Maintain system governance, data security, user access, and compliance.

Develop scalable system architectures and integrations to support multi-region expansion.

Evaluate and implement new tools.

 Revenue Process & Architecture

Design, document, and continuously improve revenue workflows (lead lifecycle, routing, scoring, opportunity management, handoffs, renewals).

Create automation to reduce manual work and increase operational efficiency.

Collaborate with GTM leaders to ensure processes reflect best practices and business strategy.

Enforce CRM data quality standards through hygiene processes, validation rules, and governance.

Partner with Head of Revenue Ops and analyst to support accurate forecasting, pipeline visibility, and leadership reporting.

Ensure consistent GTM metric definitions across the organization.

 Cross-Functional Partnership

Serve as the primary systems partner for Sales, Marketing, and Customer Success.

Translate business requirements into scalable technical solutions.

Collaborate closely with Finance, IT, and Product teams on revenue-impacting initiatives (usage data, billing, product integrations).

 Enablement & Support

Training the global GTM teams on system capabilities, workflows, and new features.

Develop documentation, SOPs, and change management plans.

Handle escalations and manage system support queues.




You will be a great fit if you have:

4 years’ + experience in Revenue Operations, Business Systems, or similar roles in a B2B SaaS environment.

Strong experience with CRM architecture (e.g., Salesforce, HubSpot).

Understanding of system integrations, APIs, and middleware (e.g. Workato, Zapier, etc.).

Familiarity with sales engagement and call intelligence platforms (e.g. Apollo, Gong, Outreach, Clari, or similar GTM platforms).

Experience supporting multi-region revenue teams.

Analytical mindset with the ability to translate complex data into insights and action.

Strong attention to detail and a process-oriented approach to problem-solving.

Familiarity with revenue forecasting processes and data modelling.

Excellent communication skills and ability to collaborate cross-functionally.

Personal drive to improve current skillsets or expand into new domain areas.

Comfortable working in a high-velocity environment with competing priorities.

Excellent communication skills and ability to work with stakeholders across functions and regions.

Reporting to the Head of Risk & Compliance, this position is accountable for managing operational risks arising from operations within the Enterprise Services division. The Operational Risk Lead is responsible for ensuring the effectiveness, compliance, and integrity of activities across the Customer Care, Technology, and Marketing business units. This role involves executing risk management and assurance activities, ensuring adherence to company policies, legal obligations, and industry standards.

 

Let’s look at some key responsibilities:


Ensure adherence to Enterprise Risk Policies and frameworks by undertaking assurance activities and providing valuable insights and trends to appropriate governance forums.

Ensure effective maintenance of risk registers and profiles, including risk identification, execution of RCSA’s and resolution of self-identified, internal, and external audit issues.

Partner with Senior Managers to ensure business risks are identified and managed. Where risks have materialised (risk events/incidents), ensure they are captured and remediated efficiently, including conducting thorough root cause analysis.

Undertake independent testing of control portfolios to ensure compliance with relevant obligations and industry standards. Partner with stakeholders to address control gaps and implement operational efficiencies.

Provide high-quality risk reporting to business leadership and risk committees.

Drive continuous strategic initiatives that strengthen and enhance the risk management position of the Enterprise Services division, including problem resolution, event root cause remediation, and operational improvements.

Conduct detailed risk assessments and update frameworks and relevant policies and procedures to support regulatory/ process changes, outsourcing arrangements, and technology or business initiatives.

Develop and deliver the Line 1 assurance plan, ensuring alignment with higher-risk areas and regulatory obligations.

Establish and monitor Key Risk Indicators (KRIs); analyse trends and escalate breaches promptly.

Provide high-quality risk reporting to business leadership and risk committees.

Lead proactive assessments to identify process improvement opportunities, including streamlining, automation, and the design or enhancement of controls to strengthen resilience and efficiency.

Perform any other duties as may be reasonably required from time to time.




 We're looking for the following: 


Relevant tertiary qualifications or demonstrated equivalent knowledge through prior work experience.

5+ years’ experience in Operational Risk Management, Internal Audit, or Quality Assurance practices and programs.

Technology, Operations and Marketing experience banking or financial services environment, with a solid understanding of core processes workflows and customer journeys within the financial services industry.

Strong understanding of regulatory frameworks and compliance obligations relevant to consumer finance / financial services across Australia and New Zealand. 

Demonstrated expertise in control design, testing, and uplift initiatives including obligation registers and mapping.

Experience leading assurance reviews and thematic risk assessments to identify and address systemic issues.

Strong analytical, critical thinking, and problem-solving skills.

Excellent communication and report writing skills, with an ability to translate technical issues into business-friendly language.

Proficiency in interpreting complex process maps, data flows, system controls, and operational workflows.

Strong ability to collaborate with cross-functional teams, with excellent communication skills and the capability to influence and articulate complex risk scenarios.

Relevant Operational Risk Management experience within the Australian financial services industry.

Knowledge of, and experience with, risk management systems, including Protecht.
